body.title: Add confidence (-1 to 1) column to clan.memories and Memory model
body.result: confidence (-1 to 1) column added to clan.memories and Memory model
body.description:
Every memory in Igor carries a binary implicit assumption: it is either trusted or not. Shankar's lens: nothing should be binary — confidence should be a first-class float. Add `confidence FLOAT` (nullable, -1.0 to 1.0) to clan.memories and the Memory dataclass. -1 = actively harmful/wrong, 0 = unknown/unvalidated, +1 = highly reliable. Null = not yet assessed (legacy entries). Update cortex.store() and MemoryStore.write() to accept optional confidence. Update read/search paths to return it. No existing callers changed by this ticket.

**HIGH inertia pre-approved by Akien 2026-05-21 (in-session: 'ticket all of that and let's go').**

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/models.py (Memory dataclass), wild_igor/igor/memory/memory_store.py (write/read), migration SQL for clan.memories
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (Postgres only, safe migration under concurrent reads)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: schema, model, store API. Out of scope: setting confidence values on existing memories, any caller that writes habits or palace nodes.
**Test plan:** New memories can be written with confidence; existing memories have null confidence after migration; MemoryStore.read() returns confidence field; confidence outside [-1,1] raises ValueError.
